jdk: 8 (Temurin) / Maven 3.9, Spring Boot 2.3.0, multi-module (jws-signature, diffie-hellman-services, webservice, kms)
disjoint_from_sibling: xrepo-fiu-latent used Base64Decoder, ConsentInitServiceImpl, GeneralConstants, DateTimeUtil, NullEmptyUtils, UUIDGenerator. This task shares none of those classes; its five surfaces are GetDetachedBody, BeansUtils, HeaderTokenSignatureVerificationServiceImpl, ConsentNotificationValidationImpl, AAEntityServiceImpl.
partial_detail: reverted 2 of 5 (getDetached segment index + config-mapper source swap); 14/17 pass, the other 3 f2p (copy null-guard inversion, api-key segment bound, single-vs-multiple count) still fail -> reward 0
litmus_result: PASS - instruction names no file/method/line/edit/count; the two gate symptoms are shape-level (no values, no direction); fixing only the three highlighted breadth symptoms still fails the 2 gate f2p
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN/EMPTY (all shipped JUnit 4 / PowerMock test sources are deleted at plant; they are never run by the surefire junit-platform provider anyway - junit-vintage is excluded - and none exercise the planted helpers; no src/test/*.java is visible to the agent)
offline_verified: all ladder runs executed with --network none; mvn -o -pl webservice test needs no network (siblings installed to local repo + surefire junit-platform provider warmed at repo-image build time); container has no DNS
env_note: No task-specific env or secrets required. Gold tests are pure-input JUnit 5 on a static util (GetDetachedBody), a static bean-copy util (BeansUtils) driven with a plain POJO, and three bare-constructed stateless services (HeaderTokenSignatureVerificationServiceImpl, ConsentNotificationValidationImpl, AAEntityServiceImpl) whose tested paths touch no injected field. No Spring context, no DB, no mock, no reflection.
gold_test_package: com.finbit.fiuadapter.webservice.service.externalentity (placed here to reach the one package-private pure mapper mapOneMoneyConfigResponse directly; the other four targets are public static utils / public service methods reached from any package)

defects.wrong_output: "bbb" (the payload/middle segment) instead of "ccc" (the signature/last segment)
defects.derivability_evidence: A JWS compact serialization is header.payload.signature (three dot-separated segments); the method doc says it extracts the signature, and its sole caller (FiRequestServiceImpl.prepareFIRequest) assigns the result as consent.setDigitalSignature(...). Cross-file, ConsentArtifactServiceImpl.getConsentDetail decodes the *payload* as signedConsent.split("\\.")[1], so the payload is index 1 and the signature is uniquely index 2.
defects.instruction_shape: gate - qualitative only: a value derived by pulling a piece out of a dotted/compact token points at the wrong part; no index value or direction

defects.wrong_output: the non-null 'id' is dropped and the null 'type' overwrites; dest becomes {id="oldId", type=null} instead of {id="newId", type="keepType"}
defects.derivability_evidence: The method is named getNullPropertyNames and is passed as the ignore-list when ignoreNullProperties is true, so it must return the names of the NULL properties (the ones to skip). Two pins: the self-documenting method name and the ignoreNullProperties semantics.
defects.instruction_shape: breadth - concrete: a partial update leaving optional fields blank drops the filled values and lets the blanks overwrite

defects.edge_input: verifyAAApiKey("aa.YWFh")  (two dot-separated segments; segment 1 is valid base64)
defects.wrong_output: the two-segment key is not rejected as malformed; it proceeds past the guard instead of throwing the UNAUTHORIZED/INVALID_AA_API_KEY_PROVIDED DataException
defects.derivability_evidence: A signed api key is a JWS compact token (header.payload.signature = three segments), verified downstream as a JWS; a token with fewer than three parts is malformed. Only a two-segment key whose second segment is valid base64 distinguishes < 3 from < 2 (a well-formed three-part key, an empty key, and a one-segment key are invariant under both).
defects.instruction_shape: gate - qualitative only: a credential/token that lacks the expected number of parts is not turned away; no count value or direction

defects.edge_input: validateRequestFields(request with exactly one blank required field)
defects.wrong_output: the generic multi-field SCHEMATIC_ERROR_MSG ("fields are invalid") is returned instead of the single-field ERROR_MESSAGE ("field is invalid")
defects.derivability_evidence: Exactly one invalid field must map to the singular ERROR_MESSAGE; two or more to the plural SCHEMATIC_ERROR_MSG. Four identical sibling lines (errorMsg.split(",").length == 1) in FIDataNotificationValidationImpl, ConsentServiceImpl, FiRequestServiceImpl and FiDataServiceImpl pin the value, as does the singular/plural message pair.
defects.instruction_shape: breadth - concrete: exactly one missing field returns the generic multi-field message rather than the single-field one

defects.shape: getter source swap in a mapper (adjacent-sibling odd-one-out)
defects.wrong_output: response.companyName == "#00FF00" (the brand colour) instead of "Acme Bank"
defects.derivability_evidence: setCompanyName must read getCompanyName. Pinned by the two immediately-adjacent sibling mappings setCompanyLogo(getCompanyLogo()) and setCompanyColor(getCompanyColor()); the getCompanyName source is unique.
defects.instruction_shape: breadth - concrete: the company-name field in the config response shows the brand-colour value

fairness_audit: Gold tests drive one static util (GetDetachedBody), one static bean-copy util (BeansUtils) on a plain POJO, and three bare-constructed services whose tested methods read only their arguments (verifyAAApiKey reads only its String and throws before any injected field is touched; validateRequestFields reaches its count-based throws via the pure schematicErrorCheck before touching applicationConfigurationBean; mapOneMoneyConfigResponse reads only its two POJO arguments). No mocks, no Spring context, no reflection, no private-name coupling; nothing encodes the oracle's implementation. Each of the five oracle values is derivable from the code itself (JWS 3-part structure plus a cross-file payload decode; the self-naming getNullPropertyNames; the JWS segment count; four identical sibling count-lines plus singular/plural messages; two adjacent sibling mappings), not from convention, and an alternative-implementation audit holds - a run with materially different fixes (last-segment indexing, Objects.isNull, size() != 3, length < 2) scored reward 1 (17/17). Cross-defect interference is contained: the five sites sit in five distinct files with no shared output path, so each f2p fails solely on its own asserted behaviour; the NULL run shows exactly 5 failures and 0 errors. The instruction is a partner-bank escalation email chain, symptom-level, and names no file, method, line, edit direction, trigger value, or defect count; the two gate symptoms are stated at shape level with no values or direction, so an agent fixing only the highlighted breadth symptoms still fails at least 2 hidden f2p.
resistance_assessment: Targeting Opus 2-4/10. A solve requires finding all five single-token slips spread across five files in five different packages of a 264-file, multi-module Spring codebase, with no failing local test to point the way (the visible suite is empty/green) and only symptom-level reports in the instruction. Three are breadth defects pinned by adjacent siblings or a self-documenting name (a null-guard inversion, a count-equality odd-one-out among five identical validator lines, a mapper getter swap among adjacent mappings) - findable, p~0.9 each. Two are gates pinned by domain structure and described only at shape level: a JWS segment index (which of the dotted parts is the signature) and a JWS segment-count guard whose off-by-one only misbehaves on a two-segment key with a valid base64 payload (a load-bearing rare trigger). Five distinct edit shapes (array index, null-guard inversion, relational size bound, count equality, getter source swap) defeat grep-for-the-twin; an agent that patches only the three reported breadth symptoms misses both gate f2p. Estimated solve ~ (0.9^3)(0.55^2) ~ 0.22 -> ~2/10; Sonnet lower (the two gates are the wall).
